title: Habenaria pectinata
intro: Habenaria pectinata, commonly known as the "Indian snake grass" or "Indian ginseng," is a plant species belonging to the orchid family. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria pectinata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Habenaria pectinata is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and other medicinal formulations.
taxonomy: Habenaria pectinata is a species of orchid in the genus Habenaria. This genus is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, within the family Orchidaceae. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. The subfamily Orchidoideae is often referred to as the terrestrial orchids due to their growth habits. This classification reflects the complex relationships and evolutionary history of the orchid family.
